pages.raw_text:
The franchise question will be
threshed out by two debating clubs
made up of boys at the Y. M. c. A.
next Thursday night. The ques-
.tion is, Resolved, That the c., M.
& P. S. and North Coast railroads
should be admitted to Spokane
without requiring them to promise
terminal rates."


Census enumerators are making
a canvass of the lodging houses and
hotels today with a view of enumer.
ating those who are making their
homes in those places. For this
purpose enumerators from all out
lying sections of the city have been
called in.


The Spokane Merchants associa.
ton has received notification that
the Spokane freight rate case will
be taken up April % and a decision
rendered early in May.


John SpaIding, building superin.
tendent for the Great Northern
railroad, has returned from Melon
aid lake, 80 miles east of Kalispell,
Mont. where he has been oversee
ing the building of a new summer
home for James ]. and Louis w.
Hill.


Orthodox and reformed Jews will
enter upon the festival of the Pass
over tonight. and the services will
continue for a week. Services will
be held tonight in the Temple
